metadata.description: Public cross-platform (Claude Code + Codex) plugin marketplace for smorinlabs

plugins.name: explain
plugins.description: Concrete-anchored explanation shorthand — /explain answers in a fixed anatomy: what it is, just-enough context, a real before/after example, and the payoff — with options/deeper/steps modes inferred from the target.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: factor-harness
plugins.description: Architecture-aware review, refactor, and cross-implementation dedup — surfaces bugs, smells, and precedent gaps, then emits specs for Superpowers to execute.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: guided-research
plugins.description: Orchestration layer over the built-in /deep-research skill — decides when deep research is worth doing, shapes the prompt with project constraints, invokes the engine, and organizes results into a durable, reusable research tree.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: project-harness
plugins.description: Lightweight PROJECTS.md project management — capture, scope, decompose, triage, and drift-audit projects as auto-triggering skills that hand execution off to Superpowers.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: repo-hygiene
plugins.description: Repo & release-readiness checks — CI/pre-commit audit, version drift, README sync, manual test guides, and a PR merge flow that resolves every review thread before merging.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: use-html-theme
plugins.description: Themed-HTML toolkit, two pure skills. use-html-theme: on any HTML-generation request, offer a catalog of visual themes (Birchline warm-editorial, Technical-minimal docs-site, High-contrast-dark dashboard), apply the chosen theme to all subsequent HTML in the session, and optionally persist it per-project — natural language and [theme: x] inline-flag control, side-by-side preview on request. html-codesign: interactive 'pick and export' decision pages — every question opens with a collapsible context-and-recommendation preamble (free-form rich content on a clarity scaffold), the reader answers, skips, or raises a question on each section, folds answered sections to dense summary rows for review, then exports the decision as Markdown/JSON (slim by default, full ADR-style by toggle) with stable IDs for diffable iteration. Portable across Claude Code and Codex.
plugins.author: Steve Morin
